summary: Iteration 2 verification — auth refactor to httpOnly-cookies-only (no localStorage). Backend regression: 23/23 pytest cases pass. Frontend auth flow verified end-to-end: (1) login lands on /admin, (2) page reload preserves admin session via httpOnly cookies, (3) localStorage contains NO 'noir_token' (only PostHog analytics), (4) Abmelden logs out and redirects to /admin/login, (5) direct /admin nav while logged out redirects to /admin/login. Cookies present: access_token + refresh_token. Public site: home shows new light commercial design (white bg, burgundy accents, 6× 'Heute verfügbar' green badges, WhatsApp green button in header). /models renders 6 cards in 4-column grid at lg. /escort/hamburg, /services/luxury-escort-hamburg, /blog/, /kontakt all load with proper German titles. Contact form (TEST Iteration 2) submitted successfully — 'Vielen Dank' success state shown. No console errors beyond the expected /api/auth/me 401s on public pages; no React unmount warnings; the previously-noted empty warning from iteration_1 no longer appears.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.js correctly switches to httpOnly cookies only; useMemo on context value and useCallback on login/logout are good React patterns and prevent stale-closure rerenders.
api.js uses axios.create with withCredentials:true — required for ingress to forward cookies cross-origin. Verified working over the preview HTTPS domain.

AuthProvider always probes /auth/me on mount which causes one 401 for anonymous visitors. Acceptable per the request; could be skipped via a sessionStorage 'authed' hint to silence the console error, but it's purely cosmetic.
server.py still ~670 lines — consider routers split (carried over from iteration 1 review, no blocker).
Logout dashboard briefly shows 0/0/0 counts because admin pages refetch data with no cookies before the route guard redirects; cosmetic flash only, redirect fires within ~200ms.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of tested flows (5/5 auth scenarios + 5/5 public pages + contact submit)

seed_data_creation: One TEST_ contact ('TEST Iteration 2', test-it2@example.com) created via /kontakt during this run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth refactor verified. httpOnly cookies access_token + refresh_token are set on /api/auth/login with samesite=none/secure=true and ride along on axios withCredentials:true. localStorage no longer holds noir_token. Regression file: /app/backend/tests/test_noir_hamburg.py (uses access_token from JSON body for Bearer header — this is fine because login response still returns the token even though the frontend ignores it). If main agent considers dropping access_token from /api/auth/login response body to harden against accidental future XSS leaks, the pytest fixture admin_token will need to switch to cookie-based auth.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found in iteration 2.
